DISCOVERY 302, MOTION TO DISMISS ACTION, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE TO COMPEL PLAINTIFFS DEPOSITIONS AND DISCOVERY RESPONSES IS GRANTED-IN-PART AND DENIED-IN-PART. AS ACKNOWLEDGED IN BOTH THE PLAINTIFFS' OPPOSITION AND DEFENDANTS' REPLY, PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOW SERVED VERIFIED DISCOVERY RESPONSES, AND PLAINTIFF ISRAEL BLASQUEZ WAS DEPOSED ON OCTOBER 18, 2016, RENDERING THOSE PORTIONS OF DEFENDANTS' MOTION MOOT. WHILE PLAINTIFFS HAVE PROVIDED SEVERAL EXCUSES FOR FAILING TO MAKE RAFAEL BLASQUEZ AVAILABLE FOR DEPOSITION, THE TRIAL IN THIS CASE IS SCHEDULED FOR DECEMBER 2016. ANY FURTHER DELAY IN CONDUCTING THIS KEY DEPOSITION WOULD BE PREJUDICIAL TO THE DEFENDANTS. ACCORDINGLY, THE COURT ORDERS THAT PLAINTIFFS MAKE RAFAEL BLASQUEZ AVAILABLE FOR DEPOSITION NO LATER THAN NOVEMBER 15, 2016. IF RAFAEL BLASQUEZ IS INCARCERATED DURING THIS TIME FRAME, PLAINTIFFS ARE FURTHER ORDERED TO COORDINATE WITH THE RELEVANT JAIL TO ARRANGE FOR THE DEPOSITION, AND ENSURE THAT IT OCCURS AS SOON AS POSSIBLE. THE COURT DENIES DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO DISMISS, AS TERMINATING SANCTIONS ARE HIGHLY DISFAVORED, AND THERE IS NO EVIDENCE OF WILLFUL DISCOVERY ABUSE. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE PRO TEM: DIANA KRUZE; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; NOT REPORTED; DEPT. 302.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Mar-14-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
John Henning, of Law Office of Brian Larsen, Tel. 415.398.5000, for plaintiffs;
Melissa Wood, of Murchison & Cumming, Tel. 415.524.4307, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling, but modifies as to the sanctions award. DEFENDANT DEBBIE NGO, STEVEN J. MERLO TRUST, 750 CAFE, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted only as to the Trust and Ms. Ngo, and the Cafe's alternative motion for summary adjudication is denied as to both the second and third causes of action. The Trust is entitled to summary judgment because plaintiffs acknowledge that the Trust had no role in operating the cafe and did not control the premises at the time of the incident. (Response to UMF 6.) There are triable issues regarding Ms. Ngo and the Cafe's liability for both the negligent hiring and retention claim and the general negligence claims. There is a triable issue whether Mr. Yep and Mr. Vo were employees of Ms. Ngo and the Cafe. Ms. Ngo testified that her husband was going to pay Mr. Yep by taking him out for a meal. (Ngo Depo. 64:4-65:3.) Ms. Ejanda, an employee of the Cafe, testified that Mr. Vo would sit behind the cash register and serve beer to patrons. (Ejanda Depo. 18:3 -19:3, 72:2-4.) There is a triable issue whether Ms. Ngo and the Cafe knew or should have known of Mr. Yep and Mr. Vo's unfitness to act as employees. The April 21, 2013 police report authenticated by Officer Lucas states that on that date there was an incident at the cafe where a gun was brandished. While some of the report contains inadmissible hearsay, other portions of the report are admissible. The report is excepted from the hearsay rule by the public employee records exception. Officer Lucas wrote the report within the scope of his duty as a public employee, the report was made near the time of the events in question, and the source of the information contained in the report indicate its trustworthiness. (Evidence Code 1280.) Officer Lucas states that he encountered Mr. Vo at the premises, interviewed him, and found a black replica handgun in the cafe's refrigerator. Officer Lucas also discussed the incident with Ms. Ngo. Ms. Ngo stated that the police could seize the gun. The statements by Ms. Ngo are admissible per Evidence Code 1220. There is also a triable issue whether Mr. Vo and Mr. Yep were acting within the course and scope of their agency. Plaintiffs have presented sufficient evidence that the misconduct of Mr. Vo and Mr. Yep were reasonably related to the tasks they were asked to perform and was reasonably foreseeable in light of their responsibilities and the prior incident. (Responses to UMF 7-14 and 22-24). Plaintiffs' request for sanctions per CCP 128.5 is granted because at her deposition, Ms. Ngo contradicted her own declaration in several material respects (Ngo Depo. 81:2 -84:7) and the police report also contained significant contradictions with her declarations. These contradictions show that substantial portions of the motion are frivolous within the meaning of section 128.5. Plaintiffs do not seek a specific amount in sanctions. Plaintiffs are awarded $500. against Ms. Ngo, the Cafe and their counsel. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

Notice Of Motion To Dismiss Action, Or In The Alternative To Compel Plaintiffs Depositions And Discovery Responses
Off Calendar Oct-31-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. DEFENDANT DEBBIE NGO, STEVEN J. MERLO TRUST, 750 CAFE, INC. Motion To Dismiss Action, Or In The Alternative To Compel Plaintiffs Depositions And Discovery Responses is GRANTED-IN-PART and DENIED-IN-PART. As acknowledged in both the Plaintiffs' opposition and Defendants' reply, Plaintiffs have now served verified discovery responses, and Plaintiff Israel Blasquez was deposed on October 18, 2016, rendering those portions of Defendants' motion moot. While Plaintiffs have provided several excuses for failing to make Rafael Blasquez available for deposition, the trial in this case is scheduled for December 2016. Any further delay in conducting this key deposition would be prejudicial to the Defendants. Accordingly, the Court orders that Plaintiffs make Rafael Blasquez available for deposition no later than November 15, 2016. If Rafael Blasquez is incarcerated during this time frame, Plaintiffs are further ordered to coordinate with the relevant jail to arrange for the deposition, and ensure that it occurs as soon as possible. The Court denies Defendants' motion to dismiss, as terminating sanctions are highly disfavored, and there is no evidence of willful discovery abuse. Prevailing party shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge Pro Tem: Diana Kruze; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302.
